judgment.summary: Dell Technologies exhibits strong growth and improving profitability, but faces valuation concerns and a tight current ratio.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter (Jan 31, 2026) EPS of $3.89 beat estimate by 10.7%. Next earnings date is May 28, 2026.
positives: Revenue growth of 39.5% YoY indicates strong top-line momentum.
Earnings growth of 57.3% YoY and quarterly earnings growth of 47.4% show significant profitability expansion.
Forward P/E of 15.91x is substantially lower than trailing P/E of 27.07x, suggesting future earnings expectations are more attractive.
PEG ratio of 0.99 suggests the stock is reasonably valued relative to its expected earnings growth.
Free cash flow of $6.61B TTM provides substantial operational cash generation.
negatives: Profit margin of 5.2% is relatively low, indicating efficiency challenges.
Current ratio of 0.91 and quick ratio of 0.59 suggest potential short-term liquidity constraints.
Total debt of $32.26B is significant relative to total cash of $11.53B.
Analyst mean target of $203.09 is below the current spot price of $235.26, implying limited upside according to analysts.
Beta of 1.06 indicates slightly higher volatility than the broader market.

examples.body: $DELL still looks like one of the more mispriced names in the AI hardware stack. Everyone is focused on pure-play AI software, but the real revenue ramp is hap…
$SPY $AAPL pulls their head out of their Ass and comes up with an $AI strat with $PLTR !!! $DELL knows!!!
